contentBody: Which part of the CSS box model is always transparent and creates space between elements?
answerExplanation: Margin is always transparent and creates space outside the element.

contentBody: What does the 'box-sizing: border-box' property do?
option.2: Includes padding and border inside width
answerExplanation: border-box ensures total width includes padding and border.

contentBody: Which property is best used when you want an element to grow with content instead of fixing its height?
answerExplanation: min-height allows flexible growth based on content.

contentBody: Which of the following are characteristics of block elements? Select ALL that apply.
answerExplanation: Block elements take full width, start new line, and respect spacing properties.

contentBody: Which of the following statements correctly differentiates padding and margin?
option.1: Padding is inside, margin is outside
option.2: Margin is inside, padding is outside
answerExplanation: Padding is inside the border; margin is outside.

contentBody: What is the purpose of media queries in CSS?
option.3: To apply styles based on screen size
answerExplanation: Media queries enable responsive design by adapting styles.

contentBody: Which statements about media queries are correct? Select ALL that apply.
option.4: They respond to screen size changes
answerExplanation: Media queries evaluate conditions and apply styles dynamically.

contentBody: Which CSS position value keeps an element fixed relative to the viewport?
answerExplanation: fixed positioning keeps element attached to viewport even on scroll.
